codeReview.summary: The dashboard replaces the placeholder with a substantive admin surface: it fetches the catalog and health endpoints, renders aggregate stats, ingestion health, coverage gaps, and a filterable/sortable source table. I found one accuracy issue in the filter-tab counters, but the page still satisfies the milestone contract overall.

issues.description: The status-tab counters are computed from the raw `catalogData.feeds` list, while the table rows themselves are filtered from `mergedFeeds` after overlaying fresher `/api/health` status data. When the health payload disagrees with the catalog snapshot, the badge counts on the `OK` / `Empty` / `Failed` tabs can diverge from the rows users actually see after filtering.
issues.suggestedFix: Derive the tab counts from `mergedFeeds` (or from the same merged status source used by `filteredFeeds`) so the counters and filtered table always describe the same dataset.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The scrutiny-validator workflow needs a documented manual-review fallback when `scrutiny-feature-reviewer` Task launches fail.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: All three Task launches for this polish scrutiny run exited before producing a report because the subagent tried to use `custom:GPT-5.4-(XHigh)-3`, so the validator had to complete the feature reviews manually.

summary: Pass. The admin dashboard delivers the required source-health and ingestion-health UI, with one non-blocking issue in the tab counters when health data is fresher than the catalog snapshot.
